title: Fields for indexes are not present anywhere  in metadata/information schema
description:
### What happens?

When querying `duckdb_indexes()` the fields belonging to the index are not present.

According to this link: https://discord.com/channels/909674491309850675/921073327009853451/1273234124177408051
The fields should be present under `expressions` but is not.



### To Reproduce

```
create table test_index (id integer primary key, name varchar, age tinyint, class int);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX test_idx ON test_index (name, age, class);

select * from duckdb_indexes() WHERE table_name ='test_index' ;
```

### OS:

MacOS Sonoma

### DuckDB Version:

v1.0.0 1f98600c2c

### DuckDB Client:

Command Line

### Full Name:

Yuv

### Affiliation:

N/A

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE employee (emp_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, emp_name VARCHAR, emp_age TINYINT, emp_salary DECIMAL(10, 2));
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX emp_idx ON employee (emp_name, emp_age);
INSERT INTO employee (emp_id, emp_name, emp_age, emp_salary) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30, 70000.00);
CREATE TABLE department (dept_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, dept_name VARCHAR, budget DECIMAL(15, 2));
key_bug_features: Index fields missing in duckdb_indexes() query results
